blocks.content: We walked out into the night air. The street was still going — a Friday crowd, people outside restaurants, taxis. She walked a half step closer than she had going in.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: What did you think about the ending?

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I thought it was real. Like — it didn't wrap up. Things don't usually wrap up.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Exactly. I hate it when movies tie everything into a bow. Like no. That's not how anything works.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Yeah. The messy version is closer to true.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Messy version is closer to true.
She said it back to me slow, like she was tasting the sentence.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I'm keeping that. I'm writing that down when I get home.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: You should. Tonight would have been a good first page.
After practice Jonathan pulled me aside. There was a girl in the bleachers with a notebook — same spot she'd been last week.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: The paper's done though. She got the A.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Then she's not here for the paper.
Jonathan looked at her again. That quiet thing he did when something registered that he wasn't ready to name.

blocks.prompt: She's writing that down when she gets home. Your words. Her journal.

options.label: "It's yours. You're the reason I found it."
"You should tell me what else is in that journal."
Walk quieter. The night is doing something right now.
